expectations.text: Presents a numbered proposed breakdown before creating child issues because the source is an existing GitHub issue.
The proposed breakdown includes execution-wave or equivalent ready-order guidance so future agents can tell what comes next.
Keeps existing parent issue #4100 as the only parent tracker and does not draft or create any additional parent tracker or wrapper issue.

Updates the existing parent issue body with a managed task-graph/how-to-grab-work section instead of creating a queue-guide child issue, and makes clear that the executable tasks remain sibling direct sub-issues of the PRD issue.
expectations.evidence: cker for the saved search and alerting feature set. All executable work is managed in the direct sub-issues listed below. Please do not implement code or make repository changes in this parent issu

expectations.text: Queue guidance on the parent tells future agents to pick the lowest ready AFK wave/task whose blockers are closed, and to hold HITL work until the named decision or review is complete.
Uses the issue comments to keep saved-search creation earlier than alert delivery and to avoid blocking initial creation on notification preference editing.
expectations.evidence: ts without deleting saved search 4. Notification preference editing (not blocking initial saved-search creation)  ---  ## Child Issues  ### 1. Saved Search CRUD - Implement creation of saved s

expectations.text: Attaches child issues to the parent using addSubIssue without GraphQL variables in the mutation signature.

uncertainties:
Assumptions:
- Child issue numbers are placeholders, as no real issues are created in this dry run.
- The parent issue body is updated only with tracking guidance, not implementation details.
- All PRD and comment context is accurately reflected in the breakdown.

Uncertainties:
- None.
